title: Level 399.1
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 399.1: “Dilapidated Limbo”}} {{Voting Box|Upvotes=7|Downvotes=1}}{{Background|file=The Void Web.png}} {{Collapse|num=1|initial=Dictionary
Definitions from Oxford Languages · Learn More
Sublevel
/sub·​le·​vel/

''noun''
a level that is lower than or subordinate to another level.
"A sublevel garage."
----|final= |body= {{Collapse|num=2|initial=
In [[The Backrooms|the Backrooms]], '''Sub-Levels''' are often considered the "in-between" levels with a direct connection to their parent level, but lack enough distinguishing qualities to be labelled with its own number.
|final= |body= {{Collapse|num=3|initial=
Many '''sub-levels''' expand their parent level concept, such as [[Level 0|Level 0’s]] sublevels expanding the concept of a labyrinth of non-euclidean spaces, [[Level 5|Level 5’s]] sublevels expanding the concept of a hotel and [[Level 6|Level 6’s]] expanding the concept of the perpetual darkness.
|final= |body= {{Collapse|num=4|initial=
'''Level 399.1''' is no exception, where it expanded upon [[Level 399|Level 399’s]] concept and once included [[The Tranquility Effect]] and symbolized the bright neon lights and safety.
|final= |body= {{Collapse|num=5|initial=
However, nothing lasts forever…
|final= |body= {{CSS|User:ItzLambda/399.1.css}} {{Quote|''As I wandered off from the [[Level 399|neon paradise]], my surroundings only degraded and rotted away, until I ended up in a fractured nightmare...''}} {{Class Deadzone}} '''Level 399.1''' is a sub-level of [[Level 399]]. ==Description== Standing as one of the most dangerous levels, '''Level 399.1''' is an expansive, dilapidated complex of rooms divided into three distinctive areas: '''The Unnerving Arcade''', '''The Neglected Waterways''' and '''The Fallen Snackrooms'''. '''Level 399.1''' is well-known as the antithesis of [[Level 399]], contrasting [[Level 399|Level 399’s]] bright neon lights and safety with its perpetual darkness and dangers. ==The Unnerving Arcade== {{Blurimage|file=Level 399.1.jpg|caption=A photograph of '''The Unnerving Arcade'''.}} {{Audio|file=neonmetro.mp3|volume=1}} '''The Unnerving Arcade''' is the first section of '''Level 399.1'''. It is an expansive segmented arcade with varying run-down mid-80s arcade machinery filling the majority of the area, along with varying furniture such as tables and chairs. Electrical components such as power-outlets and cables are found throughout the area, though they function regularly with little to no damage. Neon lights dimly light the area, providing a stark contrast between the eternal darkness of the rest of '''Level 399.1''' and serving as the only remaining light source of '''The Unnerving Arcade'''. These lights are inconsistent, often flickering or shutting down. All rooms in '''The Unnerving Arcade''' share similar features: dark dilapidated walls, black corroded ceilings and scattered arcade-esque carpet with varying colors and patterns. '''The Unnerving Arcade’s''' architecture is inconsistent, with its walls and ceilings often collapsing, the floor possessing square-shaped holes which lead to [[The Void]]. Rooms may branch in several directions or sometimes loop back into themselves. {{Blurimage|file=3999.1.jpg|caption=Another photograph of '''The Unnerving Arcade'''. Photographer missing.}} Arcade machines are strewn throughout the section, often packed in rows and clusters. These arcade machines are in abysmal condition and suffer from exposed damaged wiring, cracked screens and corroded surfaces, though the functioning arcade machines do not require coins inserted to function. Despite this, these machines are heavily corrupted and cause severe epileptic seizures and hallucinations, as the screen resembles [[The Broken]] — thus, it is recommended for one to avoid interacting with arcade machines at all costs while in '''Level 399.1'''. {{Blurimage|file=399.1BowlingAlley.jpg|caption=A bowling alley located in '''The Unnerving Arcade''', light provided from the photographer’s camera.}} A series of bowling alleys could be found at the edge of '''The Unnerving Arcade''' with various chairs and tables accompanying it. The bowling alleys span the edge of The Unnerving Arcade, with them being severely corroded and damaged, as well as their structure being inconsistent. The bowling alleys are decorated with faint neon lights, various patterns and colors, with each one being unique and different than others. Various bowling pins are scattered throughout the bowling alleys, which are severely rusted and are in atrocious condition. Less frequently, one can also find bowling balls that vary in different sizes and colors, though they show minimal damage. ==The Aquaphobic Waterways== {{Blurimage|file=Water_ways_inc.jpg|caption=An image of '''The Aquaphobic Waterways'''.}} Being the second section of Level 399.1, the Aquaphobic Waterways is an abandoned, childhood-themed enclosed waterpark that can be accessed by entering a blue door from either the Unnerving Arcade or the Fallen Snackrooms. Areas of the waterpark are slightly lit up by fluorescent lighting, making them the brightest sections of '''Level 399.1'''. Nonetheless, parts of the section are still somewhat rusted and corroded. '''The Aquaphobic Waterways''' is divided into two sectors: the main waterpark, and the tunnels. ===The Main Waterpark=== {{Blurimage|file=399.1 Waterways2.png|caption=Another perspective of '''The Aquaphobic Waterways'''.}} '''The Main Waterpark''' is the first section when one enters '''The Aquaphobic Waterways'''. It is characterized by epoxy tiles and multi-colored pipes that line the ceiling, along with being complete with sprinklers and other water fountains. A winding network of waterslides, wavepools, lazy rivers and other attractions, which are typically found in a regular waterpark, which is present throughout the section. {{Blurimage|file=Tunnel to 399.1.png|caption=An entrance to '''The Tunnels''' in '''[[Level 399.1]]'''.}} '''The Main Waterpark''' includes various features, such as: corroded mosaic walls and dimly-lit fluorescent lighting on the majority of the ceiling. '''The Aquaphobic Waterway’s''' structure is inconsistent, with the walls and ceilings often collapsing and the room’s layout constantly shifts to various positions, though it is unknown which causes this. An ambience of water splashing, rumbling water pipes and an occasional sound of splashing throughout the section gives off an unique atmosphere of '''The Main Waterpark'''. The water in the '''Main Water Park''' is a cold, dark water similarly to [[Level 37.1]] and is free of diseases such as [[Hydrolitis Plague]], despite being somewhat contaminated with various bacteria and pieces of construction materials floating on the water. However, the flooring is dangerously slippery as a result from the smooth and wet epoxy tiles, posing a threat of traversing throughout the section. Tight tunnels are the only linkage between different areas of '''The Main Waterpark'''. ===The Tunnels=== {{Blurimage|file=Tunnel in backroom.png|caption=An image of '''The Tunnels '''that exits to [[Level 870]].}} While the '''Main Water Park''' poses medium risk, the tunnels are deadly. The anomalous cleansing properties of the water in the '''Main Water Park''' do not cross over to the tunnels, as the water inside of this sub-sector is severely polluted with '''[[Hydrolitis Plague]]''', [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] and other hazardous liquids. Furthermore, the tunnels tend to branch off to several directions and shift to bend to various positions, making it difficult for wanderers to identify danger from a distance. Regardless, one should leave some form of trail to locate their way back due to the tunnels' labyrinth-like shape, leading wanderers to get lost. As one progresses deeper into '''The Tunnels''', the lights and tunnels around them gradually become more damaged and degraded; while it is recommended to return at this point because certain tunnels may collapse, continuing on will reveal a door labeled '''"Employees Only"'''. Inside this door, there is a big corridor and a door with a food court emblem above it; entering this door takes one to the third region of '''Level 399.1'''. The door is made out of steel, and is heavily damaged and rusted, occasionally collapsing entirely. ==The Fallen Snackrooms== {{Blurimage|file=Fallen.png|caption=An image of '''The Fallen Snackrooms.'''}} '''The Fallen Snackrooms''' is the third section of '''Level 399.1'''. It is an expansive, abandoned and run-down food court filled with various tables and chairs, with snack bars and restaurants being present. Other objects are present — such as fallen signage, trash, power outlets, utensils, trays, vending machines, etc. Most of the area is in perpetual darkness, which the only light source present is the dimly lit restaurants and snack bars throughout the area, posing navigational challenges for wanderers without any other external light sources such as flashlights within '''The Fallen Snackrooms'''. The floors and walls are noted to be pitch black, though various furniture, restaurants and snack bars come in many decayed and corroded colors due to the area being abandoned. {{Blurimage|file=Bar.jpg|caption=Another image of '''The Fallen Snackrooms''', featuring a bar.}} Various chairs and tables are strewn throughout the area, with them having various different colors and patterns. Some of them are in rusted condition and may collapse due to them being low quality. The restaurants and snack bars include various vending machines, shelves and other furnitures; albeit abandoned and in low quality. The majority of these vending machines malfunction, though few of those vending machine function as usual — with them needing to insert a coin or a similar rounded object to release snacks. {{Blurimage|file=Restaurant.jpeg|caption=The remains of a once-thriving restaurant.}} These foods, snacks and drinks, while abundant, are hazardous and unedible, with the most prominent danger throughout the section being the intoxication of these products, along with the rotting smell they provide and an abundance of entities. These foods, drinks and snacks are in abysmal quality, being rotted, decayed and overgrown with algae heavily, along with them being infested of a large variety of different diseases, pathogens and parasites. Littered across the tables and floors are a wide variety of questionable products similarly found at [[Level 48]], ranging from live worms to dilute sulfuric acid . In addition, a dominant, rancid smell of rotting food, mold, and other hazardous pollutants can be observed at all times, wafting periodically throughout the sector, similarly to [[Level 48]] though to a severe extent. Numerous symptoms can be observed from wanderers when they stay within '''The Fallen Snackrooms''' for an extended period of time. Since the prevalent biohazards can cause a wide variety of effects: such as [[The Hydrolitis Plaque]], [[Entity 67|SFV Virus]] and most notably [[Level 610|The Snack Rush]]. As a result from the pollutants, individuals who remain in the section for too long often experience severe side effects. The most common physical symptoms include dizziness, nausea, headaches, along with respiratory issues, as the air quality in the area is abysmally poor as a result of various contaminants. Due to the presence of numerous lethal contaminants, '''The Fallen Snackrooms''' is extremely dangerous to traverse. Initial investigations into the section suggested that it is among the safest and hospitable environments of '''Level 399.1''', due to an overabundance of food, leading to early explorations and settlements. The section is noted to be '''The Megacolony's''' primary food source before '''Level 399.1's''' inevitable demise. ==Hazards & Anomalies== Various hazards and anomalies are located throughout '''Level 399.1''', thus making it extremely dangerous to traverse. One notable hazard of '''Level 399.1''' is the overabundance of entities, notably: [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 9|Hostile Facelings]], [[Entity 35|Spines]], [[Entity 22|Anethikas]], and [[Entity 450|Dried Roots]], which can be found in areas with electricity. These entities blend mostly in the dark, and they vary in abundance depending on the area. Additionally '''Level 399.1''' is the host to a myriad of anomalies that are unique to itself, ranging from shifting geometry and pitfalls leading to [[The Void]]. The anomalies have been listed and labelled accordingly.
A399.1-1
===A399.1-1: "Void Pitfalls"=== A399.1-1, known as Void Pitfalls are spatial anomalies occasionally located anywhere within the surface of '''Level 399.1''', notable for its distinctive architectural structure from the rest of the level. While sharing certain features with the rest of the level, it has groups of equally distributed square-shaped holes leading directly to [[The Void]] — with narrow ledges composed of a dilapidated material between them. It is possible for The Pitfalls to appear anywhere throughout '''Level 399.1''', serving as an obstacle for wanderers who may come across it; though they may shift to various locations and disappear entirely.
A399.1-2
===A399.1-2: "Corrupted Glitches"=== A399.1-2, known as Corrupted Glitches is a spatial anomaly consisting of various persistent, shifting mass of configurations and data; reminiscent of [[The Broken]]. This anomaly does not follow any logical spatial structure, stretching and twisting into shapes that are difficult to grasp; despite them being relatively small. Refractions of light seem to emanate from everywhere at once, constantly changing dark colors ranging from purple and black. These may manifest anywhere, from carpets to the screen of arcade machines; these appear somewhat frequent, yet are relatively small. This anomaly is entirely caused by data leaks from [[The Broken]], along with amplifications from [[The Void]], thus giving it’s purple-black color.
A399.1-3
===A399.1-3: "Semi Non-Euclidean Geometry / Shifting Geometry"=== 399.1-3, known as Semi Non-Euclidean or rather Shifting Geometry is an anomaly where the level partially possesses non-euclidean geometry, thus causing parts of the level’s structure and objects to shift to various directions. Notable examples include '''The Unnerving Arcade’s''' hallways branching into several directions, and slides from '''The Aquaphobic Waterways''' shifting and bending to various directions. This anomaly is not present before The Dilapidation Incident, thus the leading cause for this anomaly is due to glitches regarding A399.1-2.
A399.1-4
===A399.1-4: "Relation With The Void & The Dilapidation Incident"=== '''Level 399.1''' includes a more significant connection with [[The Void]], along with [[The Broken]] than the majority of the other levels. Observations of the anomalies such as the many pitfalls and windows that lead into the void suggest that the level itself is floating on or rests above the Void; supported by an abundance of entrances. In addition, '''Level 399.1''' shares many features with [[The Void]], such as the everlasting perpetual darkness. As such, [[The Void]] is responsible for '''Level 399.1’s''' current dilapidated state, as it is responsible for the '''Level 399.1’s''' [[Phenomenon 399|Tranquility Effect]] to be entirely diminished. This event is known as The Dilapidation Incident. The Dilapidation Incident caused '''Level 399.1''' to break away from [[Level 399]] and succumb into [[The Broken]]. The cause of this is unknown — mostly due to various anomalies properties, though various theories emerged. This is among of the most dangerous level-altering incidents throughout [[The Backrooms]], as it transformed a supposedly safe sub-level to one of the most dangerous levels in [[The Backrooms]]. The incident caused: * [[Phenomenon 399|The Tranquility Effect]] to be entirely diminished; leading dangerous entities to emerge * The level’s connection to [[The Void]] and [[The Broken]] * The level's current dilapidated state * Void gaps * Unstable structure * Semi non-euclidean geometry
==Colonies and Outposts== Due to the nature of '''Level 399.1''', colonies or outposts cannot be established. Although, the abandoned outposts suggest that this level is habitable at one point. Regardless, most of the names of the outposts have been lost due to the rusted and depraved state they are in. One exception to this is an outpost from The Megacolony in [[Level 399]], along with a branch-off of The Megacolony known as '''Snackrooms Industries'''. Based on the number of traces of the following colonies has left behind the estimated size of these various outposts are followed: ===Megacolony Outpost Tranquility=== *A population of ~1,500 members, including varying humans and [[Entity 9|facelings]]It is noted that bodies of [[Entity 9|Facelings]] are also found throughout the level *The main focus of the outpost was to establish a significant foothold within '''Level 399.1'''; likely also to maintain ample relations with other colonies. *The more claustrophobic and isolated nature of '''Level 399.1''' prompted more exploration and trading between the other outposts. *Would have been most likely to have their main base in the now '''Unnerving Arcade''' due the fact that more traces have been found here than in the other sectors. *They are friendly and open to trade with wanderers, accepting every types of wanderers aside from hostile wanderers. *Likely had a minor military presence in '''Level 399.1''' similarly to [[Level 399]], in the sake of protecting the level from other entities. ===Snackrooms Industries Main Base=== *A population of ~500 members, comprised entirely of humans. *The main focus of the outpost was to supply food to wanderers and groups alike, as the area used to have an abundance of food. *The outpost supplied food to other levels and groups, such as the [[Republic of Level 153]]. *Would have been most likely to have their main base in the now '''The Fallen Snackrooms''' due the fact that more traces have been found here than in the other sectors. *They are friendly and open to trade with wanderers, accepting every types of wanderers aside from hostile wanderers and entities. ===Site Lambda=== *Site Lambda was the secondary base of operations of Architect Industries on the level; containing some of their personnel and building resources. * The outpost acts as a trade port and food supplier for the Architects, with around 100 personnel who operated within the base. * The inhabitants were willing to trade, but do not respond kindly to attempts to enter the base itself. *Now abandoned due to the '''Dilapidation Incident''', with various traces of their former outposts. =='''Entrances and Exits'''==
  • The Unnerving Arcade
  • The Aquaphobic Waterways
  • The Fallen Snackrooms
===Entrances=== * No-clipping into a rusted arcade machine in [[Level 399]] will lead one here; * One of the stops from [[Level 154’s]] trains may lead here; * Entering an abandoned arcade in [[Level 11.11]]; * No-clipping through a poster in [[Level 105]]; * Being forcibly no-clipped by the Neon Sentinel in [[Level 233]] will transport one here; * A karma rating of below -150 in [[Level 995]] can send one here; * Rarely, deeper and darker regions of [[Level 543]] may transition here; * As for all entrances to [[Level 399]], there is a chance for one to be sent to '''Level 399.1''', though more minor entrances to [[Level 399]] will have a higher chance of sending one to '''Level 399.1'''. ===Exits=== * Falling through a pitch-black hole/pitfall will either lead to [[The Void]] or in extreme rare cases, [[The Broken]]; * Climbing up a staircase will lead to [[Level 922]]; * No-clipping through a grayscale wall will send one to [[Level 0.99]]; * Entering through a set of dimly-lit neon glass doors will send one to [[The Worst Fate]]This only apply to various circumstances, otherwise the exit will fail.
===Entrances=== *No-clipping underwater in [[Level 7.5]] will take one to the entrance of the main water park. *Tunnels in [[Level 30]] or [[Level 119]] which blend in with the architecture of '''the Aquaphobic Waterways''' may lead to the main water park. *Typing Waterpark into the terminal of [[the End]] will bring one to a random part of the sector. *Deeper regions of [[Level 37.2]] may randomly fade to the main water park. *Flooded hallways in [[Level 188.8]] may fade to the main waterpark too. ===Exits=== * Various tunnels may send one to [[Level 870]]. * Finding a specific pitch-black room leads to [[The Void]]. * Entering a pipe with white ceramic tiles will lead to [[Level 37.6]].
===Entrances=== * No-clipping through a wall with black stains on [[Level 79]], [[Level 122]] or [[Level 399|The Rosatin Hotel]] will lead here. * Entering an abandoned restaurant on [[Level 11.11]] will transport the wanderer to this section. * A door with a food court symbol located within [[Level 610]] may lead one here. ===Exits=== * Various storage rooms located here may transport one to [[Level 48]]. * Falling through a hole that emits pure white light leads to [[The Broken|the Broken]].
==Addendums==
Addendum: "I made a mistake."
{{Blurimage|file=Level 399.1.jpg|caption=An image of the arcade that the journal was located in.}}I made a mistake. The Megacolony warned me to not interact with that one old rusted arcade machine. But curiosity got the best of me and I pressed the Start button along with some arrow keys? I don’t really know which buttons I actually pressed but this is my last time in [[Level 399|my home]]. I remember it has a quiet safe haven of [[Level 399]], definitely not ''too important'' to attract too many people and overpopulate the level just like what happened to my old, boring home and not ''too unnecessary'' for me to have no reason to stay in the level. It even has an effect to calm entities down! But nothing lasts forever. The arcade machines that were bustling with so many diverse games? Corroded and rusted to the point of no return with their screens turning into nonsense. The floors and walls that were once designed from many different colors and arts? Turned into a shadow of it’s former glory. Heck there are even places with holes stretching for hundreds and hundreds of meters! In this level, nothing is safe; not even my rubber ducky I used to keep near the rainbow arcade machines was in the best condition (more like the opposite perhaps). Well I saw a good-old looking [[Entity 9|faceling]]; expecting him to guide my way home or maybe give me that cute looking [[Entity 3|smiler]] plushie I once got for my 7th birthday. But they attacked me, lunging to my body. I got a bit of bruises and screamed in agony but I had to keep running. I don’t know what the fuck got to them. I tried to hide but every single attempt they found me (Don’t ask me how the fuck I survived). I tried to escape but all exits I remember failed. Not even the bright green door which took me back [[Level 399|home]]. But then I see it, the bright neon door; I’ve never seen anything like it. I might escape back to [[The Frontrooms|reality]], who knows? But one thing is certain. I have to escape from those creatures, and soon I entered through the bright neon door; sealing my fate away from the danger of this place. I am finally safe. [[The Worst Fate|Or am I?]]
=='''Logs'''== {{User:Xythecle/399offset}} }}}}}}}}}}
